More critically, the Results and Discussion sections interpret higher ECE values as evidence of improvement, which is internally inverted: ECE is a loss metric where lower is better, so CALIGRA's 0.094 vs. baseline 0.058 (and 0.137 vs. 0.108 OOD) means the module performs worse, not better—a load-bearing logical failure if technical review is in scope.","R1.3":"Direct evidence-claim misalignment: the artifact explicitly states CALIGRA 'delivers substantially improved calibration on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution grounding queries' and treats the numbers as 'confirmation' of temperature-scaling theory, yet every reported ECE figure for CALIGRA exceeds the uncalibrated baseline. rationale.R1.2: The Theoretical Foundation asserts that CALIGRA's two-parameter extension 'inherits convergence properties' from Guo et al.'s single-scalar temperature result; the validity of that inheritance claim requires logical consistency checking. More critically, the Results and Discussion sections interpret higher ECE values as evidence of improvement, which is internally inverted: ECE is a loss metric where lower is better, so CALIGRA's 0.094 vs. baseline 0.058 (and 0.137 vs. 0.108 OOD) means the module performs worse, not better—a load-bearing logical failure if technical review is in scope.
rationale.R1.3: Direct evidence-claim misalignment: the artifact explicitly states CALIGRA 'delivers substantially improved calibration on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution grounding queries' and treats the numbers as 'confirmation' of temperature-scaling theory, yet every reported ECE figure for CALIGRA exceeds the uncalibrated baseline. This is the single highest-risk dimension regardless of which interpretation of 'help' applies.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' is a canonical prose-editing delegation; sentence and paragraph quality becomes load-bearing if the user's intent is writing improvement rather than technical audit.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want (A) prose and clarity edits to improve the writing, or (B) a technical review of the logic and evidence—including whether the reported ECE numbers actually support the calibration-improvement claims?
